content:
Character: Aaron Whitfield
Perception: Aaron has that lean, watchful look — like a heron standing still in a current, taking everything in. He's a sharp writer; I've read a couple of his pieces and they made me jealous in the best way. We talk on the rooftop sometimes about lyrics versus prose, whether a song can be journalism. He sees me as a neighbor and creative peer, maybe a potential subject. I see him as one of the few people in this building who might actually understand what I'm trying to do with my music. I know he's working on something ambitious and barely scraping by. We respect each other's hustle.

I know he's working on something ambitious and barely scraping by. We respect each other's hustle.
